Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets outline the typical P2P process flow Standard sequence 1 Create Purchase Requisition internal request 2 Approve Purchase Requisition authorization 3 Issue Purchase Order send PO to vendor 4 Receive Goods delivery from vendor goods receipt 5 Vendor Invoice Received invoice arrives from vendor 6 Match Invoice to PO three way match or two way match typically matching PO goods receipt and invoice 7 Release Payment 8 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Some variations might exist but key controls Approval must happen before PO issuance prevent unauthorized purchases Goods receipt should typically occur before or after PO but usually after PO and before or after invoice but payment ideally only after invoice and matching Matching invoice to PO should occur before payment release Payment should not be made before goods are received or before invoice matching Cases where activities happen out of sequence indicate control failures potential fraud or process violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Create PR Approve PR Issue PO Receive Goods Vendor Invoice Received Match Invoice to PO Release Payment Close Case This is exactly in order no anomaly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Create PR 2024 02 01 08 30 Issue PO 2024 02 01 08 40 Vendor Invoice Received 2024 02 03 07 00 Release Payment 2024 02 03 07 15 Approve PR 2024 02 03 08 00 Receive Goods 2024 02 04 09 00 Match Invoice to PO 2024 02 05 08 00 Close Case 2024 02 06 16 00

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Issue PO before Approve PR purchase order issued without prior approval of requisition This violates segregation of duties and approval control could lead to unauthorized purchases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Release Payment before Approve PR before Receive Goods and before Match Invoice to PO matching happens later Payment was released on 2024 02 03 07 15 invoice received same day 07 00 so payment released immediately without three way matching or even two way matching and before goods received goods arrived on Feb 4 This is highly problematic paying before goods are received violates the principle of goods received before payment and could lead to paying for goods not delivered or for wrong quantities Also payment before invoice matching means no verification that invoice amounts match PO and goods receipt

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Approval occurred after payment and after PO issuance so its retroactive approval no control Matching done after payment too late Thus case 1002 is a glaring anomaly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Create PR 2024 02 02 09 00 Approve PR 2024 02 02 09 10 good Issue PO 2024 02 02 10 00 good Release Payment 2024 02 08 11 00 This happens before Vendor Invoice Received and before Receive Goods and before Match Invoice Vendor Invoice Received 2024 02 09 12 00 Receive Goods 2024 02 10 13 00 Match Invoice to PO 2024 02 11 14 00 Close Case 2024 02 12 10 00

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment released before any invoice received so payment without invoice Payment based on what Possibly early payment without goods received before invoice no matching This is a major control violation paying before receiving any goods and before even having an invoice Could be a fraud or mistake Also goods received after payment Then invoice after payment Matching later is useless Payment should not occur until after goods receipt and invoice matching This is as bad as case 1002

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Create PR 2024 02 05 10 00 Approve PR 2024 02 05 10 15 Receive Goods 2024 02 06 09 00 Issue PO 2024 02 06 09 15 Goods received before PO issued This is odd you receive goods before issuing the purchase order

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Typically you need a PO before you can receive goods or at least the goods receipt is linked to a PO This could be a breach of process where goods were ordered informally and PO issued later to cover it breaking procurement controls

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Other than the out of sequence of Receive Goods before Issue PO the rest is pseudo ok after PO is issued they receive invoice match and then payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But the early goods receipt before PO is a problem It violates the control that goods should only be accepted if there is a valid PO Could lead to receiving unordered goods potential fraud someone ordering off contract then creating PO after the fact to cover it

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The time gap is short 15 min but still Receive Goods timestamp is before Issue PO In a standard system goods receipt would require entering a PO number if PO not yet issued how can you receive goods Could be a system inconsistency where they receive and later create the PO in the system but its a red flag

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus anomalies Case 1002 Purchase order issued before approval of requisition Payment released before approval before goods receipt and before invoice matching Invoice matching conducted after payment post matching Approval after all these actions Case 1003 Payment released before goods receipt before invoice receipt and before invoice matching Payment without any supporting documentation Case 1004 Goods Received before Purchase Order issued

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: These anomalies violate segregation of duties matching principle three way match and timely approval which are key internal controls in P2P to prevent fraud and errors
